definition: The maximum amount the plan will use to calculate payment for a covered service.
When an out-of-network provider bills you for the difference between their charge and the plan allowed amount.
The percentage of covered costs you pay after meeting any applicable deductible.
A fixed amount you pay for a covered service.
The amount you pay before the plan begins paying for services that apply to the deductible.
A family member covered under the subscriber's health plan.
A document that explains how a claim was processed and what the member may owe.
The identifier for the employer or group plan associated with your coverage.
A provider or facility that contracts with the plan's network.
A period when eligible members can enroll in or change health plans.
The most you pay for covered in-network services in a plan year.
Primary care provider. This clinician is often the first point of care and may coordinate referrals.
Approval required before some services are covered.
A life change such as marriage
column5: or loss of coverage that may allow plan changes outside open enrollment.
